commit.message:
Clean AutomaticDestinations jump lists and notify shell

Windows 11 File Explorer's Recent view reads from AutomaticDestinations
jump list files (OLE compound files), not just .lnk shortcuts. Deleting
the .lnk alone leaves phantom entries visible in Explorer.

- Add JumpListCleaner using OpenMcdf to parse and clean jump list entries
- Clean jump lists during both startup scan and real-time watcher events
- Add SHChangeNotify P/Invoke to refresh Explorer after cleanup
- Add CountMatches for TestRules to show jump list match count
- Refactor ShortcutResolver.ResolveTargetFromBytes for reuse by JumpListCleaner
- TestRules now reports both .lnk and jump list matches

commit.message:
Fix bg carving offset when zoomed or panels open

Root cause: clientToSvg() used manual math assuming the viewBox
maps 1:1 to the workspace div. When side panels resize the workspace,
the SVG's preserveAspectRatio adds letterboxing, making coords wrong.

Fixes:
- clientToSvg() now uses getScreenCTM().inverse() for robust
  screen-to-SVG conversion that handles all layout scenarios
- zoomBy() uses clientToSvg() for pivot calculation
- ResizeObserver on workspace keeps viewBox in sync when panels
  open/close, preserving center and zoom level

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: optimize aggregateTree path/id construction and node name sorting (#2205)

`aggregateTree` performs avoidable string/array work on every shard row:
repeated `join`/`slice`/`join` for path IDs and a `localeCompare` sort
on `nodeNames` per insertion.

- **Incremental ID construction**: Build node IDs from `parentId` via
template literal instead of accumulating `pathSegments` and re-joining
on each iteration
- **Deferred nodeNames sort**: Append unique names unsorted during
aggregation, sort once in the final post-pass

```diff
- const id = pathSegments.join("|")
- const parentId = index === 0 ? null : pathSegments.slice(0, index).join("|")
+ const id: string = parentId ? `${parentId}|${info.segment}` : info.segment
+ parentId = id

- return [...existing, nextName].sort((a, b) => a.localeCompare(b))
+ return [...existing, nextName]
  // ...final pass:
+ node.nodeNames.sort((a, b) => a.localeCompare(b))
```

~26% median improvement on the synthetic benchmark (320 → 236 ms). All 8
`StorageExplorerPage` component tests pass unchanged.

* feat(nexkit-file-watcher): add file watcher service for .nexkit directory protection

Implements NexkitFileWatcherService that monitors the .nexkit/ directory for
external file changes. When a user manually modifies a managed file, the
service rolls back the change and prompts for confirmation. Changes made
internally by Nexkit are suppressed to avoid false positives.

Features:
- FileSystemWatcher for .nexkit/**/* with content cache for rollback
- Per-path suppression (suppressPath) for individual file operations
- Bulk suppression (beginBulkOperation/endBulkOperation) for batch operations
- Rollback + user confirmation on file change/delete
- Warning on manual file creation in managed directory
- Singleton pattern for easy access from any service

Integration:
- TemplateFileOperations: suppress before install/uninstall
- BackupService: bulk suppress during delete/restore
- NexkitFileMigrationService: bulk suppress during migration
- ServiceContainer: registered and disposed
- extension.ts: watcher started on activation

Co-authored-by: decarufe <1083536+decarufe@users.noreply.github.com>

Also update serviceContainer.test.ts to verify the new service.

fix: load SSH env-secrets and restore zsh Alt keybindings (#118)

* fix: load codespace env-secrets in zsh and bash SSH sessions

When connecting to a codespace via SSH, environment variables from
/workspaces/.codespaces/shared/.env-secrets were not loaded for zsh
and bash shells. This caused missing tokens, PATH entries, and other
codespace-injected env vars.

Add codespace-env blocks (mirroring the existing codespaces.fish fix)
to ~/.zshenv, ~/.bashrc, and ~/.bashenv that:
- Read and base64-decode each entry from .env-secrets
- Merge PATH by appending missing entries (preserving shell-managed priority)
- Export all other variables directly
- Only activate during SSH sessions (SSH_CONNECTION check)
- Use marker comments for idempotent re-runs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- CustomDomainService.detect(): guard against both siteUrl AND apiUrl hostnames
  so /public/contest-by-domain is never called on the main ctf.rootaccess.live
  site (eliminates spurious 404 in browser console on every page load)

- contest-manager loadDomainStatus(): reconstruct DNS instructions (CNAME +
  TXT records) from persisted domain status when domain is set but unverified
  and domainSetResponse is null — prevents instructions disappearing on reload

- contest-manager saveDomain(): strip http(s):// prefix from domain input
  before submitting; show warning toast so user knows it was auto-corrected

- GetDomainStatus handler: return 500 for DB/internal errors, only 404 for
  actual sql.ErrNoRows — prevents cold-start Lambda DB failures appearing as

Increase encryption key generation from 128-bit to 256-bit (#255844)

Generated encryption keys were 128-bit (16 bytes → 32 hex chars), which
falls short of current security standards. Keys should be 256-bit to
align with AES-256 requirements.

## Changes

- **`src/cli/encryption_keys/encryption_config.js`**: Updated
`_generateEncryptionKey()` to use `crypto.randomBytes(32)` instead of
`crypto.randomBytes(16)`, producing 64-character hex strings (256-bit
keys)

```js
// Before
return crypto.randomBytes(16).toString('hex');  // 128-bit, 32 hex chars

// After
return crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('hex');  // 256-bit, 64 hex chars
```

This affects all three generated keys:
`xpack.encryptedSavedObjects.encryptionKey`,
`xpack.reporting.encryptionKey`, and `xpack.security.encryptionKey`.
Existing configured keys are unaffected — only newly generated keys will
use the increased length.

* Fix: format

* Fix: ambiguous Timer reference in SystemsPage.xaml.cs:20.

The using System.Timers; directive brought System.Timers.Timer into scope, but System.Threading.Timer was also available (via implicit usings), causing CS0104. The XAML compiler error was a cascade from that.

Fix applied: removed using System.Timers; and fully qualified the three Timer usages as System.Timers.Timer.

* Fix bug in dotnet-build.ps1

Bug in Invoke-Step in dotnet-build.ps1. It checks $LASTEXITCODE after running each step, but when the step is a pure PowerShell script (like dotnet-version.ps1), no native executable runs, so $LASTEXITCODE retains whatever stale value existed from a previous terminal command. Adding $global:LASTEXITCODE = 0 before & $Action ensures each step starts clean.

commit.message:
fix(orleans): R5 final audit — cap SettingsBroadcast registrations, add [PreferLocalPlacement] to singleton orchestrators, replace cross-grain validation throws with log-and-return

- SettingsBroadcastGrain: Add MaxRegisteredAssets=200 hard cap on
  _registeredAssets ConcurrentDictionary to prevent unbounded growth
  from buggy clients, API replays, or activation storms

- SchedulerGrain + PortfolioMakerGrain: Add [PreferLocalPlacement] to
  singleton orchestrator grains with persisted state and reminders.
  Default random placement caused unnecessary cross-silo latency on
  every reminder tick from 22+ asset grains

- MLTrainingGrain.SubmitGradientsAsync: Replace ArgumentException /
  ArgumentOutOfRangeException throws with LogWarning + early return.
  Raw throws serialized cross-silo, deserialized by caller, then caught
  generically with a vague log. Now logs the specific validation failure
  locally and returns silently, avoiding expensive exception round-trips

Fix/bicep errors (#13)

* fix: Bicep compilation errors and linter warnings

- Fix multi-line string in @description decorator (single-line now)

  and redis connection string outputs with #disable-next-line

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add local deploy script for fast infra iteration

deploy-local.ps1 validates Bicep, provisions, and deploys using
local Azure CLI credentials. Supports -ValidateOnly, -SkipProvision,
and custom environment names. Much faster than GitHub Actions for
iterating on infrastructure changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(infra): comprehensive security hardening of all Bicep templates

- Disable local auth: Storage (allowSharedKeyAccess:false, allowBlobPublicAccess:false),
  Key Vault (enablePurgeProtection:true), App Insights (DisableLocalAuth:true),
  Redis (aad-enabled:True)
- MI-based connectivity: Replace key-based AzureWebJobsStorage with
  AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName; add Storage Blob Data Owner, Storage Account
  Contributor, and Storage Queue Data Contributor role assignments
- Private endpoints: Add VNet with delegated functions subnet and PE subnet;
  create private DNS zones for blob/queue/table/vault/redis; create private
  endpoints for Storage (blob, queue, table), Key Vault, and Redis
- Network isolation: Set publicNetworkAccess:Disabled on Storage, Key Vault,
  and Redis; add networkAcls with Deny+AzureServices bypass
- Function App hardening: VNet integration with vnetRouteAllEnabled, http20Enabled,
  remoteDebuggingEnabled:false, detailedErrorLoggingEnabled:false,
  WEBSITE_RUN_FROM_PACKAGE:1, FUNCTIONS_EXTENSION_VERSION:~4
- Redis upgrade: Basic C0 → Standard C1 for private endpoint support
- Remove secret outputs: Drop instrumentationKey, storage/redis connection strings
  from outputs; mark Redis connection string @secure() for inter-module passing
- Document FIC permission requirements (Contributor + User Access Administrator)
- Add @description decorators to all parameters and outputs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: switch to Flex Consumption (FC1) plan for Azure Functions

- Replace Y1/Dynamic SKU with FC1/FlexConsumption in function-app.bicep
- Add functionAppConfig block for deployment, scaling, and runtime config
- Deployment via blob container with SystemAssignedIdentity auth
- Scale: 100 max instances, 2048 MB instance memory
- Runtime: dotnet-isolated 8.0 (configured via functionAppConfig, not linuxFxVersion)
- Split premium-only app settings (FUNCTIONS_WORKER_RUNTIME, EXTENSION_VERSION,
  WEBSITE_RUN_FROM_PACKAGE) from common settings shared by both plans
- Add deployment blob container to storage.bicep (created when not using premium)
- Add Storage Table Data Contributor role to storage-access.bicep
  (required when allowSharedKeyAccess is false)
- Generate deterministic deployment container name in main.bicep

implies a plan tier. It now accurately describes what it controls:
VNet integration, private endpoints, private DNS zones, and disabled
public network access on storage/KV/Redis.
